message:
Add shell-aware command tokenizer, propagate build ID for remote cancellation, and fix async concurrency issues

- rch/hook.rs: Replace naive split_whitespace() with a proper tokenize_command()
  that handles single quotes, double quotes, and backslash escaping. Extend
  is_filtered_test_command to recognize "run" and "t" (nextest aliases) in
  addition to "test", add --profile/--config/-Z to flags_with_args, and handle
  positional arguments after the -- separator. Pass build_id to TransferPipeline.

- rch/transfer.rs: Add build_id field and with_build_id() builder to
  TransferPipeline. When set, exports RCH_BUILD_ID=<id> in the remote SSH
  command string so that remote processes can be identified and cancelled.

- rchd/alerts.rs: Replace std::thread::spawn with tokio::task::spawn_blocking
  for webhook delivery to maintain async runtime awareness and avoid blocking
  the tokio executor thread pool.

- rchd/benchmark_scheduler.rs: Refactor scheduling loop to release the
  pending_queue mutex before calling the async is_worker_eligible() check,
  preventing lock contention across await points. Re-acquire the lock only for
  the final queue removal. Also add child.wait().await after child.kill() in
  two error paths to prevent zombie processes.

- rchd/cancellation.rs: Fix pkill pattern from 'RCH_BUILD_ID={}' to
  'RCH_BUILD_ID={};' (trailing semicolon) to match the actual exported
  environment variable format in the remote command string.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
